rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning expression types with declared helper types (int, real, bool).
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only valid output-to-input relationships.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced features and variables are declared or visible in the same annex scope.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE declarations to avoid resolution errors.
Validate that binary expressions in guarantees have matching left and right operand types.
Prevent type errors by tracing root causes of '<error>' types back to unresolved identifiers or invalid declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in component implementation scopes.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, and constants; implementation annexes for assignments.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; they must be in component type annexes.
Do not reference identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current scope.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions without explicit conversion.
Do not introduce package-qualified names not present in the model's dependencies.
